meta.name: LEC-5 Life Events Checklist
meta.description: The LEC-5 is a 17-item screening tool to assess exposure to potentially traumatic events, designed as a companion instrument to the PCL-5.
meta.author: Weathers et al. (2013)
meta.reference: Weathers FW, Blake DD, Schnurr PP, Kaloupek DG, Marx BP, Keane TM. The Life Events Checklist for DSM-5 (LEC-5). National Center for PTSD; 2013.
meta.instruction: Listed below are a number of difficult or stressful things that sometimes happen to people. For each event, please indicate whether it happened to you.

questions.text: Natural disaster (e.g., flood, hurricane, tornado, earthquake)
Transportation accident (e.g., car accident, boat accident, train wreck, plane crash)
Serious accident at work, home, or during recreational activity
Exposure to toxic substance (e.g., dangerous chemicals, radiation)
Physical assault (e.g., being attacked, hit, slapped, kicked, beaten up)
Assault with a weapon (e.g., being shot, stabbed, threatened with a knife, gun, bomb)
Sexual assault (rape, attempted rape, made to perform any type of sexual act through force or threat of harm)
Other unwanted or uncomfortable sexual experience
Combat or exposure to a war-zone (in the military or as a civilian)
Captivity (e.g., being kidnapped, abducted, held hostage, prisoner of war)
Serious injury, harm, or death you caused to someone else
Any other very stressful event or experience

ranges.label: No Trauma Exposure
ranges.description: You did not report experiencing any potentially traumatic events.
suggestions: If you have other mental health concerns, professional help is still available

ranges.label: Low Trauma Exposure
ranges.description: You have experienced a small number of potentially traumatic events.
suggestions: If these events still bother you, consider speaking with a professional

ranges.label: Moderate Trauma Exposure
ranges.description: You have experienced several potentially traumatic events.
suggestions: Consider a further mental health assessment
If you have PTSD symptoms, consult a professional
Consider completing the PCL-5 to assess PTSD symptoms

ranges.label: High Trauma Exposure
ranges.description: You have experienced many potentially traumatic events. A detailed mental health evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: A comprehensive mental health assessment is strongly recommended
If needed, contact a crisis helpline (e.g., 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
